_about.schema: 7-axis 0-3 scale matching The Gin Is In: juniper, citrus, floral, heat, spice, herbal, fruited
_about.note: Confidence reflects how much the description supports the scoring. Low-confidence entries should be re-scored by Erik against tasting notes when convenient.

161.name: Jettywave Barrel-Aged Botanical Gin
161.reasoning: Shiso + nasturtium + pistachios reads herbal-floral with citrus from Meyer lemon. Juniper not mentioned as foreground; corn base is neutral. 6 months in American oak adds mild fruited/vanilla but at 40% ABV the heat stays low. Short description — confidence low.

162.name: Jettywave California Botanical Gin
162.reasoning: Description is one sentence; scored as a sibling of #161 (same distillery, garden botanicals) without the barrel-aging. Almost no specifics — re-score from tasting note when possible.

163.name: Kiss Cold Gin New York Style
163.reasoning: 'New York style' is loose marketing; 5x distilled implies clean neutral. Juniper + lemon + 'herbs' is all we have. Defaulted to balanced-low profile.

164.name: Old World Spirits Rusty Blade Gin
164.reasoning: Barrel-aged in French oak with a 12% grape eau de vie base reads as fruited + oak spice. No botanical bill in description. 47% ABV = heat 3. Treat as a barrel-aged gin archetype until better data.

166.name: Seven Stills Wood Isle Dry Gin
166.reasoning: 61-char description — almost no signal beyond 'San Francisco Bay coastal botanicals.' Defaulted to a balanced San Francisco craft profile. Seven Stills is defunct; can't re-source.

513.name: Quattro Gatti Gin Classico
513.reasoning: Detailed botanical bill: Italian juniper, ginestra (Spanish broom — floral), peperoncino (chili — heat+spice), coriander, orange, olive leaf. Reads as balanced 2s with peperoncino bumping spice/heat. Mild fruited from orange.

514.name: Quattro Gatti Olive Grove Gin
514.reasoning: Savory olive-and-artichoke profile — described as 'earthy, forest floor, rain-soaked stone.' Olive leaf + artichoke + thyme = herbal 3. Lemon peel present but not foregrounded. No floral, no fruit — this is a savory martini gin.

519.name: James Gin California Dreamgin'
519.reasoning: Mushroom + patchouli + oregano = unmistakable herbal 3 (savory/earthy). Girolle mushroom adds peppery + apricot hint (fruited 1). Juniper not foregrounded — this is a 'gin' more by category than profile.

520.name: James Gin American Mustard
520.reasoning: Mustard seed + ginger + caraway + cubeb pepper + licorice = spice 3 (the dominant axis). 'Slight sensation of fire' = heat 2. Bay + rosemary = herbal 2. Dried tomato gives a fruited hint. Juniper buried in a long botanical list.

554.name: High Mountain Desert Gin
554.reasoning: Sage-forward, 'piney,' rosemary, white pepper. Mountain sage = herbal 3. Juniper present but secondary. White pepper = spice 2. No citrus emphasis. Earthy desert character.

560.name: Renais Gin
560.reasoning: Chablis grape-skin base is the distinguishing feature — fruited 3 (white wine character). Lemon + lime = citrus 3. Linden flowers + acacia honey = floral 3. 40% ABV = heat 1. Cubeb mild. Distinctive profile that explains why TGII passing didn't matter — Renais doesn't fit a conventional gin axis well.

575.name: WildJune Western Style Gin
575.reasoning: Double juniper bill (wild Red Berry + organic common) = juniper 3, 'bold.' Cinnamon + white pepper = spice 2. Centennial hops add floral 1. Lemon balm + hops = herbal 2. Red Berry juniper carries some fruit. 45% ABV = heat 2.

576.name: WildBark West Texas Dry Gin
576.reasoning: Double juniper bill (wild Alligator + organic common) with 'earthy juniper upfront' = juniper 3. 'Pop of citrus from orange peel' = citrus 2. 47% ABV = heat 3. Spare botanical bill — coriander only spice. Rye/malt base adds backbone but doesn't show as fruited.

617.name: High Mountain Desert Barrel Rested Gin
617.reasoning: Same base as #554 (sage-forward Utah gin) rested 8mo in rye barrels. Sage stays dominant (herbal 3). Allspice + white pepper + baking spice = spice 2. Vanilla/caramel from barrel — these read as warming, not fruited. No citrus mentioned. 44% ABV.

621.name: James Gin London Drizzle
621.reasoning: Petrichor concept: 'rich juniper and tangy orange up front, then earthy beetroot and rooty botanical spice, mineral petrichor, peppercorn warmth.' Beetroot + lovage + bay = herbal 3 (earthy/umami). Ginger + peppercorn = spice 2. Beetroot adds a sweet-earthy fruited hint.

622.name: Pacific House Bay FC Poppy
622.reasoning: 'Citrus-forward brightness' + bright orange peel = citrus 3. California poppy is the signature — floral 3 ('gently floral' finish). Juniper present but supporting. 'Soft spice' + 'herbal lift' = 1 each. 43% ABV = heat 2.

625.name: Wood's Mountain Hopped Gin
625.reasoning: 'Medium juniper-forward.' Cascade hops contribute floral + citrus without bitterness — bumps citrus and floral to 2 each. Elderflower reinforces floral. Western-style frame puts herbal at 2. 45% ABV = heat 2. Distinct from IPA gins because hop bitterness is suppressed.

636.name: Big Gin Peat Barreled
636.reasoning: Big Gin's flagship is juniper-heavy LDG (juniper 3). 'Cask treatment dulls the citrus' = citrus 1. Cardamom + grains of paradise + Tasmanian pepperberry + cracked black pepper = spice 3. 47% / 94 proof = heat 3. Orris adds floral 1. NOTE: The peat/smoke character has no TGII gin axis — needs to be flagged separately if the matcher ever wants to surface it (could add a smoke axis to the schema later).
